articles.headline: World Cup final squads ranked: Of all 48 national teams, who can win this summer?
articles.description: We have the final 26-player squads that can win the FIFA World Cup, so let's go through all 48 teams and rank where they stand.

images.name: Pochettino: USMNT are moving in a good direction ahead of World Cup
images.caption: Mauricio Pochettino speaks after the USMNT's 3-2 win over Senegal ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup trophy New York [1296x729]
images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Nigeria files World Cup complaint to FIFA over Congo DR
articles.description: Nigeria are hoping to revive their hopes of appearing at the 2026 World Cup with complaint to FIFA over the use of at least nine ineligible players by Congo DR.

images.name: DR Congo vs. Nigeria [1296x729]
images.caption: Nigeria were beaten on penalties by DR Congo in their 2026 World Cup playoff.
images.alt: Alex Iwobi of Nigeria in action against Aaron Wan-Bissaka of DR Congo during the FIFA World Cup 26 CAF qualifiers play-offs final match between Nigeria and DR Congo, in Rabat, Morocco, on 16 November 2025

articles.headline: Can Tunisia cause an upset in Group F?
articles.description: Ed Dove discusses Tunisia's chances of progression from Group F as they face tough opponents Japan, The Netherlands and a UEFA play off winner.

images.caption: Ed Dove discusses Tunisia's chances of progression from Group F as they face tough opponents Japan, The Netherlands and a UEFA play off winner.

keyEvents.text: Goal! Côte d'Ivoire 1, Seychelles 0. Sébastien Haller (Côte d'Ivoire) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the high centre of the goal.
Goal! Côte d'Ivoire 2, Seychelles 0. Ibrahim Sangaré (Côte d'Ivoire) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Karim Konaté.
Goal! Côte d'Ivoire 3, Seychelles 0. Simon Adingra (Côte d'Ivoire) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Seko Fofana.
Substitution, Seychelles. Nick Labiche replaces Bertrand Esparon.
Goal! Côte d'Ivoire 4, Seychelles 0. Karim Konaté (Côte d'Ivoire) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the high centre of the goal.
Substitution, Côte d'Ivoire. Jean-Philippe Krasso replaces Sébastien Haller.
Substitution, Seychelles. Makan Cissoko replaces Stephan Ladouce.
Goal! Côte d'Ivoire 5, Seychelles 0. Seko Fofana (Côte d'Ivoire) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Karim Konaté.
Kenner Nourrice (Seychelles) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Côte d'Ivoire. Hamed Traoré replaces Franck Kessie.
Substitution, Côte d'Ivoire. Jean Seri replaces Ibrahim Sangaré.
Lucas Panayi (Seychelles) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Seychelles. Rhio Damoo replaces Gervais Waye-Hive.
Substitution, Seychelles. Majid Freminot replaces Elie Sopha.
Substitution, Seychelles. Noris Bibi replaces Imra Raheriniaina.
Goal! Côte d'Ivoire 6, Seychelles 0. Hamed Traoré (Côte d'Ivoire) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Jean Seri.
Substitution, Côte d'Ivoire. Hassane Kamara replaces Seko Fofana.
Substitution, Côte d'Ivoire. Oumar Diakité replaces Simon Adingra.
Goal! Côte d'Ivoire 7, Seychelles 0. Jean-Philippe Krasso (Côte d'Ivoire) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the centre of the goal.
Goal! Côte d'Ivoire 8, Seychelles 0. Hamed Traoré (Côte d'Ivoire) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal.
Goal! Côte d'Ivoire 9, Seychelles 0. Karim Konaté (Côte d'Ivoire) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Jean-Philippe Krasso.
